mechanism_narrative: A persistently low federal funds rate signals an accommodative monetary policy stance, which reduces the cost of capital and encourages investment, thereby supporting corporate valuations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.18 indicates very low rates
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of periods where FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market, leading to reduced consumer spending, lower corporate profits, and subsequently negative S&P 500 forward returns over a longer horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 generates positive 60-day returns in more than 40% of instances following the trigger, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Robust retail sales growth combined with low interest rates indicates strong consumer demand and supportive financial conditions, which together boost corporate revenues and investor confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500's 20-day returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) during periods where both conditions are met, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts indicates a significant contraction in the construction sector and broader economic demand, signaling a weakening economy that will negatively impact corporate earnings and S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 400 thousand units (3-month average)
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 exhibits positive 60-day returns in more than 35% of instances after HOUST drops below the specified threshold, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating inflation, as measured by CPI, erodes corporate profit margins and prompts central banks to tighten monetary policy, increasing discount rates and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500's 20-day returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10) during periods of high and accelerating CPI, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent job creation, as indicated by non-farm payrolls, reflects a healthy and expanding economy with strong consumer purchasing power, which directly supports corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns over a short horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 132151.0 indicates a large labor force
failure_condition: If the S&P 500's 5-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with PAYEMS growth over any 3-year rolling window, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production growth combined with a declining unemployment rate signals a robust economic recovery, indicating increasing demand and efficient supply-side activity, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month average growth > 0.5% AND UNRATE falls > 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 fails to produce positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances during a post-shock recovery, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A significant contraction in real GDP signals a broad economic recession, leading to widespread declines in corporate earnings expectations and investor confidence, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 30% of periods where GDPC1 growth falls below -2%, the hypothesis is falsified.
